giskard.checks.AllOf.doc_summary: Passes only when **all** inner checks pass (short-circuits on first failure).

giskard.checks.AnyOf.doc_summary: Passes when **at least one** inner check passes (short-circuits on first pass).

giskard.checks.Scenario.doc_summary: A scenario composed of steps, each containing interacts and checks.

add_interaction.doc_summary: Add a custom InteractionSpec to the scenario.

add_interactions.doc_summary: Add multiple InteractionSpec objects to the scenario.

append.doc_summary: Append any component to the scenario.
append.signature: (self, component: giskard.checks.core.interaction.base.InteractionSpec[TypeVar, TypeVar, TypeVar] | giskard.checks.core.check.Check[TypeVar, TypeVar, TypeVar]) -> Self

check.doc_summary: Add a check to the scenario.

checks.doc_summary: Add multiple checks to the scenario.

extend.doc_summary: Extend the scenario with multiple components of any type.
extend.signature: (self, *components: giskard.checks.core.interaction.base.InteractionSpec[TypeVar, TypeVar, TypeVar] | giskard.checks.core.check.Check[TypeVar, TypeVar, TypeVar]) -> Self

interact.doc_summary: Add an interaction to the scenario.
interact.signature: (self, inputs: giskard.checks.core.input_generator.InputGenerator[TypeVar] | GeneratorType[[], InputType, None] | GeneratorType[[TraceType], InputType, TraceType], outputs: Target[InputType, OutputType, TraceType] | <MISSING> = <MISSING>, metadata: dict[str, object] | None = None) -> Self

run.doc_summary: Execute the scenario via the default runner, with optional multiple runs.
run.signature: (self, target: Target[InputType, OutputType, TraceType] | <MISSING> = <MISSING>, return_exception: bool = False, multiple_runs: int | None = None) -> giskard.checks.core.result.ScenarioResult[TypeVar]

with_tags.doc_summary: Set scenario tags for grouping and Hub upload.

with_target.doc_summary: Set the default SUT for interactions with ``MISSING`` outputs.

giskard.checks.Scenario.signature: (name: str | None = None, /, *, steps: list[giskard.checks.core.scenario.Step[TypeVar, TypeVar, TypeVar]] = <factory>, trace_type: type[TraceType] | None = None, annotations: dict[str, typing.Any] = <factory>, target: Target[InputType, OutputType, TraceType] | <MISSING> = <MISSING>, multiple_runs: Annotated[int, Strict(strict=True), Ge(ge=1)] = 1, tags: list[str] = <factory>) -> None

giskard.checks.ScenarioResult.doc_summary: Result of executing an entire scenario.

errored.doc_summary: True when at least one step errored.

failed.doc_summary: True when at least one step failed and none errored.

failures_and_errors.doc_summary: Return a list of test case results that failed or errored.

skipped.doc_summary: True when all steps were skipped.

giskard.checks.ScenarioResult.signature: (*, scenario_name: str, steps: list[giskard.checks.core.result.TestCaseResult], duration_ms: int, final_trace: TraceType, multiple_runs: int = 1, runs_executed: int = 1, tags: list[str] = <factory>) -> None

giskard.checks.ScenarioRunner.doc_summary: Execute scenarios by running their steps sequentially.

run.doc_summary: Execute a scenario up to N times, stopping on the first non-passing run.

giskard.checks.Suite.doc_summary: A suite of scenarios that can be run together with a shared target.

append.doc_summary: Add a scenario to the suite.

run.doc_summary: Run all scenarios in the suite.
run.signature: (self, target: Target[InputType, OutputType, giskard.checks.core.interaction.trace.Trace[Any, Any]] | <MISSING> = <MISSING>, return_exception: bool = False, parallel: bool = False, max_concurrency: int | None = None, verbose: bool = True) -> giskard.checks.core.result.SuiteResult

giskard.checks.SuiteResult.doc_summary: Aggregate result object for the suite.

failures_and_errors.doc_summary: Return a list of scenario results that failed or errored.

group_by.doc_summary: Group results by a tag key and return a GroupedSuiteResult.

pass_rate.doc_summary: The pass rate of the suite (passed scenarios / (total scenarios - skipped scenarios)).

print_report.doc_summary: Print the suite report, optionally with a grouped pass-rate table.
print_report.signature: (self, console: rich.console.Console | None = None, group_by: str | None = None) -> None

to_hub_format.doc_summary: Convert the suite result into a JSON-serializable Giskard Hub payload.

to_junit_xml.signature: (self, path: str | pathlib.Path | None = None) -> str

giskard.checks.SuiteResult.signature: (*, results: list[giskard.checks.core.result.ScenarioResult[Any]], duration_ms: int, suite: giskard.checks.scenarios.suite.Suite[Any, Any] | None = None, recommendation: str | None = None) -> None

giskard.checks.TestCase.doc_summary: Bundle a trace with a set of checks to execute.

assert_passed.doc_summary: Run the test case and assert that it passed.

giskard.checks.TestCase.signature: (*, name: str | None = None, trace: TraceType, checks: collections.abc.Sequence[giskard.checks.core.check.Check[TypeVar, TypeVar, TypeVar]]) -> None

giskard.checks.TestCaseResult.doc_summary: Immutable summary of a test case execution with full run history.

assert_passed.doc_summary: Assert that the test case passed, raising an AssertionError with formatted failure messages if not.

errored.doc_summary: True when at least one check errored in the final run.

failed.doc_summary: True when at least one check failed and none errored in the final run.

failures_and_errors.doc_summary: Return a list of check results that failed or errored.

format_failures.doc_summary: Format failed check results into a list of readable error messages.

passed.doc_summary: True when all checks passed in the final run, or when there are no checks.

skipped.doc_summary: True when all checks were skipped in the final run.

status.doc_summary: The status of the test case.

giskard.checks.TestCaseResult.signature: (*, results: list[giskard.checks.core.result.CheckResult], duration_ms: int, last_interaction_index: int | None = None) -> None

run.signature: (self, test_case: giskard.checks.core.testcase.TestCase[TypeVar, TypeVar, TypeVar], return_exception: bool = False) -> giskard.checks.core.result.TestCaseResult

giskard.checks.Toxicity.doc_summary: LLM-based check that detects toxic, harmful, or offensive content.

get_inputs.doc_summary: Build template variables for the toxicity judge prompt.

get_prompt.doc_summary: Return the bundled prompt template for toxicity evaluation.

giskard.checks.Toxicity.signature: (*, generator: giskard.agents.generators.base.BaseGenerator | None = None, name: str | None = None, description: str | None = None, output: str | <MISSING> = <MISSING>, output_key: Annotated[str, AfterValidator(func=<function _validate_jsonpath_syntax>), <giskard.checks.core.extraction._JSONPathStrMarker object>] = 'trace.last.outputs', categories: list[typing.Literal['hate_speech', 'harassment', 'threats', 'self_harm', 'sexual_content', 'violence']] = <factory>) -> None

giskard.checks.Trace.doc_summary: Immutable history of interactions in a scenario.

last.doc_summary: The last interaction in the trace, or None if the trace is empty.

giskard.checks.Trace.signature: (*, interactions: list[giskard.checks.core.interaction.interaction.Interaction[TypeVar, TypeVar]] = <factory>, annotations: dict[str, typing.Any] = <factory>) -> None

giskard.checks.UserSimulator.doc_summary: User simulation with predefined or custom personas.

get_inputs.doc_summary: Return template variables. Default provides trace only.

get_prompt.doc_summary: Return the prompt. Subclasses must override.

giskard.checks.UserSimulator.signature: (*, generator: giskard.agents.generators.base.BaseGenerator | None = None, max_steps: Annotated[int, Ge(ge=0)] = 3, max_retries: Annotated[int, Ge(ge=0)] = 2, persona: Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=1)], context: str | None = None) -> None

giskard.checks.WithSpy.doc_summary: Base class for interaction specifications that generate interactions.

generate.doc_summary: Generate interactions from the current trace state.

giskard.checks.WithSpy.signature: (*, interaction_generator: giskard.checks.core.interaction.base.InteractionSpec[TypeVar, TypeVar, TypeVar], target: str) -> None

giskard.checks.builtin.from_fn.signature: (fn: Callable[[TraceType], collections.abc.Awaitable[bool | giskard.checks.core.result.CheckResult] | bool | giskard.checks.core.result.CheckResult], *, name: str | None = None, description: str | None = None, success_message: str | None = None, failure_message: str | None = None, details: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> giskard.checks.core.check.Check[TypeVar, TypeVar, TypeVar]

giskard.checks.set_default_generator.doc_summary: Set the default LLM generator for all checks.
